name: Omelets Creole
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, cook onion, bell pepper, and celery in butter, stirring occasionally, until tender but not brown, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in tomatoes, breaking apart with a spoon if necessary.
3. Stir in seasonings; increase heat to high and cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens, about 8 to 10 minutes.
4. Stir shrimp into tomato mixture, then reduce heat to low; simmer just until heated through, about 2 to 3 minutes.
5. Prepare omelets; while the omelet is still moist and creamy-looking, fill each with 1/2 cup hot shrimp mixture.
6. With a pancake turner, fold the omelet in half or roll; invert onto a plate with a quick flip of the wrist or slide from the pan onto the plate.
7. Top each omelet with 2 tablespoons shrimp mixture.
OMELETS
1. Mix eggs and water.
2. In a 7- to 10-inch omelet pan or skillet over medium-high heat, heat 1 tablespoon butter until just hot enough to sizzle a drop of water.
3. Pour in 1/2 cup egg mixture (the mixture should set immediately at the edges).
4. With an inverted pancake turner, carefully push cooked portions at the edges toward the center so uncooked portions can reach the hot pan surface, tilting the pan and moving the cooked portions as necessary.
